req.title: Hr-Admin deletes performance-review
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to delete a performance-review through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-023 requires an hr-admin to delete a performance-review via UI form with validation, audit trail, and proper HTTP responses. The test suite covers: (1) a positive case validating successful deletion with audit record and 200 response; (2) a negative case for unauthenticated access (401); (3) a negative case for insufficient role (403); (4) a boundary case with a non-existent review ID; and (5) a boundary case with empty/malformed payload. These cases map to Equivalence Partitioning (authenticated vs. unauthenticated; authorized vs. forbidden), Boundary Value Analysis (edge ID values), Decision Table (authentication × authorization matrix), and Negative Path coverage (all documented error pathways).

tests.name: test_hr_admin_delete_performance_review_success
actions: issue DELETE /performance-reviews/PR-2024-001 with valid Authorization header
expected: response body contains deleted entity id 'PR-2024-001'
audit record written with action='delete', actor='hr-admin', resource_id='PR-2024-001'
tests.executable:
import requests
import pytest

def test_hr_admin_delete_performance_review_success():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8080'
    headers = {'Authorization': 'Bearer <valid_hr_admin_token>'}
    review_id = 'PR-2024-001'
    
    response = requests.delete(
        f'{base_url}/performance-reviews/{review_id}',
        headers=headers
    )
    
    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert response.json()['id'] == review_id
    assert response.json()['deleted'] is True
    
    verify_response = requests.get(
        f'{base_url}/performance-reviews/{review_id}',
        headers=headers
    )
    assert verify_response.status_code == 404
    
    audit_response = requests.get(
        f'{base_url}/audit-logs',
        headers=headers,
        params={'resource_id': review_id, 'action': 'delete'}
    )
    assert audit_response.status_code == 200
    assert len(audit_response.json()['items']) > 0

tests.name: test_hr_admin_delete_unauthenticated_returns_401
actions: verify response status code and error message
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_hr_admin_delete_unauthenticated_returns_401():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8080'
    review_id = 'PR-2024-002'
    
    response = requests.delete(
        f'{base_url}/performance-reviews/{review_id}'
    )
    
    assert response.status_code == 401
    assert 'authentication' in response.json()['error'].lower()
    
    verify_response = requests.get(
        f'{base_url}/performance-reviews/{review_id}',
        headers={'Authorization': 'Bearer <valid_token>'}
    )
    assert verify_response.status_code == 200

tests.name: test_hr_admin_delete_with_forbidden_role_returns_403
preconditions: authenticated as user with role='employee' (not hr-admin)
employee is not the review subject
actions: issue DELETE /performance-reviews/PR-2024-003 with employee Authorization header
expected: no audit record created for delete action
trace: R-WEB-023: error pathway - 403 if role forbidden
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_hr_admin_delete_with_forbidden_role_returns_403():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8080'
    headers = {'Authorization': 'Bearer <valid_employee_token>'}
    review_id = 'PR-2024-003'
    
    response = requests.delete(
        f'{base_url}/performance-reviews/{review_id}',
        headers=headers
    )
    
    assert response.status_code == 403
    assert 'forbidden' in response.json()['error'].lower()
    
    verify_response = requests.get(
        f'{base_url}/performance-reviews/{review_id}',
        headers={'Authorization': 'Bearer <valid_admin_token>'}
    )
    assert verify_response.status_code == 200
    assert verify_response.json()['id'] == review_id

tests.name: test_hr_admin_delete_nonexistent_review_returns_404
preconditions: performance-review with id=PR-9999-NONEXISTENT does not exist
actions: issue DELETE /performance-reviews/PR-9999-NONEXISTENT with valid hr-admin token
expected: error message contains 'not found' or 'does not exist'
trace: R-WEB-023: boundary case - delete non-existent resource
R-WEB-023: proper error handling for missing resource
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_hr_admin_delete_nonexistent_review_returns_404():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8080'
    headers = {'Authorization': 'Bearer <valid_hr_admin_token>'}
    review_id = 'PR-9999-NONEXISTENT'
    
    response = requests.delete(
        f'{base_url}/performance-reviews/{review_id}',
        headers=headers
    )
    
    assert response.status_code == 404
    assert 'not found' in response.json()['error'].lower()
    
    audit_response = requests.get(
        f'{base_url}/audit-logs',
        headers=headers,
        params={'resource_id': review_id, 'action': 'delete'}
    )
    assert len(audit_response.json()['items']) == 0

tests.name: test_hr_admin_delete_with_invalid_payload_returns_400
actions: issue DELETE /performance-reviews/PR-2024-004 with malformed JSON body
verify response status code and validation error
trace: R-WEB-023: boundary case - invalid payload validation
